task: Create a React counter component with increment, decrement, and reset buttons. Use useState hook. Display the current count prominently. Add keyboard shortcuts: + to increment, - to decrement, r to reset. Style it with CSS modules. Verify it works by running npm start and checking the page loads without errors.
description: Create a counter component with keyboard shortcuts
expected_output: A working React counter with +/-/reset buttons and keyboard shortcuts

task: Build a todo list application in React with add, toggle complete, delete, and filter (all/active/completed) functionality. Store todos in localStorage for persistence. Add a clear-completed button. Use useReducer for state management. Ensure the app is visually clean and responsive. Verify by adding, toggling, deleting, and filtering todos.
description: Build a persistent todo list with useReducer and localStorage
expected_output: A complete todo app with full CRUD, persistence, and filtering

task: Create a custom React hook useLocalStorage(key, initialValue) that syncs state with localStorage. The hook should support SSR safety (check if window exists), handle JSON serialization, and emit storage events for cross-tab sync. Write a demo component that uses this hook to persist a form. Verify with two browser tabs.
description: Create a custom useLocalStorage hook with cross-tab sync
expected_output: A reusable useLocalStorage hook with demo component

task: Build a debounced search input component in React. It should accept an onSearch callback, delay (default 300ms), and placeholder props. Show a loading spinner while debouncing. Handle edge cases: rapid typing, empty input, clearing. Use AbortController to cancel in-flight API calls on new input. Write a demo that simulates an API call.
description: Build a debounced search input with AbortController
expected_output: A reusable DebouncedSearch component with demo

task: Create a modal/dialog component in React with portal rendering, focus trapping, keyboard (Escape to close), aria attributes, and backdrop click to close. Support custom content, title, and footer props. Animate entrance/exit with CSS transitions. Ensure it's accessible according to WAI-ARIA dialog pattern.
description: Build an accessible modal with portal, focus trap, and animation

task: Build a paginated list component in React. It should accept an array of items and a pageSize prop. Display page numbers, prev/next buttons, and show 'Page X of Y'. Support keyboard navigation (ArrowLeft/ArrowRight between pages). Handle edge cases: empty list, single page, very large page number. Style with Tailwind-like utility classes.
description: Build a paginated list with keyboard navigation
expected_output: A PaginatedList component that handles all edge cases

task: Create a theme toggle system in React (dark/light mode). Use React Context + useReducer for the theme state. Persist the preference in localStorage. Apply CSS custom properties for theme colors. Add a smooth transition between themes. Create a useTheme() hook that components can consume. Verify the theme persists across page reload.
description: Build a dark/light mode theme system with Context API
expected_output: A theme system with Context, persistence, and smooth transitions

task: Build an accordion/FAQ component in React where only one panel can be open at a time (accordion mode) or multiple (expand mode). Support keyboard navigation (Enter/Space to toggle, ArrowUp/ArrowDown to move between panels). Animate panel open/close with max-height CSS transition. Make it screen-reader friendly with aria-expanded.
expected_output: An accessible accordion component with multiple modes

task: Create a simple client-side tab switcher component in React. Render tab buttons and corresponding panels. Support controlled and uncontrolled modes. Animate tab content switching with a fade effect. Handle edge case where no tab matches (render fallback). Add keyboard navigation between tabs with ArrowLeft/ArrowRight.
description: Build a tab switcher component with animation and keyboard support
expected_output: A flexible TabSwitcher component with animations

task: Build a drag-and-drop list reordering component in React without external libraries. Use HTML5 Drag and Drop API. Support drag handles, reorder on drop, and animate items moving. Track the dragged item visually. Handle touch events for mobile. Display the current order in a read-only preview below the draggable list.
description: Build a drag-and-drop list reorder without external libs
expected_output: A drag-and-drop list with HTML5 DnD API and touch support

task: Create a real-time dashboard widget that shows the current time, date, and a countdown timer. The clock should update every second. The countdown should accept a target date/time as a prop and show days, hours, minutes, seconds remaining. Use useEffect with cleanup to avoid memory leaks. Format all output nicely.
description: Build a clock and countdown timer dashboard widget
expected_output: A dashboard widget with clock and countdown

task: Build a simple data table component in React that accepts columns config and rows data. Support sortable columns (click header to sort asc/desc), text search/filter across all columns, and a row count display. Highlight sort column. Handle numeric vs string sorting correctly. Add a loading skeleton state.
description: Build a sortable, filterable data table component
expected_output: A reusable DataTable with sorting and filtering

task: Create a form builder component in React that renders different field types (text, email, select, checkbox, textarea) from a JSON config. Implement validation with error messages. Show a real-time character count on text fields. Disable submit while invalid. Form should submit JSON of all values. Make it reusable with a formConfig prop.
description: Build a dynamic JSON-driven form builder with validation
expected_output: A JSON-driven form builder with validation

task: Build a simple React Router implementation from scratch (no react-router). Implement a Router component, Route component, and Link component. Use the History API. Render the matching route's component. Update the URL on Link clicks. Support navigation with browser back/forward buttons. Verify with a multi-page demo.
description: Build a minimal React Router from scratch using History API
expected_output: A minimal but functional React Router implementation

task: Create a virtualized list component in React that only renders visible items (windowing). Accept items array, itemHeight, and renderItem props. Support variable height items by estimating. Handle scroll events efficiently with requestAnimationFrame or passive listener. Show an Overscan count of items above/below viewport. Test with 10,000 items.
description: Build a virtualized/windowing list for 10K+ items
expected_output: A virtualized list that renders 10K items efficiently
